repo_prior_art.cite: none; nearest eid_lingam_direction_min_order_v1 is a non-Gaussian cumulant-direction result, not Gaussian-ANM score-Jacobian local geometry

published_prior_art.cite: rolland2022score gives the population SCORE leaf functional, and zhu2023sample gives a finite-sample SCORE guarantee, but neither derives the mirror-path C_m(delta)-to-Chernoff expansion

repo_prior_art.cite: none; the catalogue has no likelihood-error exponent indexed by a SCORE diagonal-Jacobian gap

published_prior_art.cite: chernoff1952measure gives the simple-product testing exponent; the added content is its calibration by the new mirror-path score-gap calculation

repo_prior_art.cite: none; no in-catalogue local causal-order decision limit for a Gaussian-ANM mirror path

published_prior_art.cite: hoyer2008nonlinear and peters2014anm establish qualitative ANM asymmetry, while ordinary LAN/Chernoff machinery supplies the pointwise elbow once the proposed geometry is established
hoyer2008nonlinear, Corollary 1, identifies the linear-Gaussian reversible boundary
zhu2023sample analyzes a particular SCORE/DNN correctness guarantee and koehler2023efficiency studies density-parameter efficiency, not this cross-fitted diagonal-Jacobian contrast
koehler2023efficiency concerns score-matching versus likelihood efficiency for density parameters, not conditional large deviations of this causal-order statistic

novelty_flags.label: Proposition 2 / L4-insufficiency converse
novelty_flags.one_line: The claimed impossibility—“L4 ... cannot certify any lambda>0 fraction”—refutes only the proposal's new template premise: neither zhu2023sample nor koehler2023efficiency is identified as using that premise or claiming the refuted exponent conclusion.

soundness_flags.label: Proposition 2 and def:rare-event-nuisance-perturbation
soundness_flags.one_line: An L4 error ||Jhat-J||_4=O_P(r_n) creates an O_P(r_n) covariance cross-term in each plug-in variance, so r_n=o(sqrt(C_m)) does not ensure sign recovery without orthogonality or r_n=o(C_m); moreover the rare-event construction never imposes M_n q_n^(1/4)=O(r_n) (nor a compatible non-exponential-rate condition), so it does not establish that the perturbed estimator remains an admissible rate realizer.

accepted_scope_caveats.label: Theorems 1–3 / honest_scope
accepted_scope_caveats.caveat: The likelihood frontier and local elbow are asserted only for the explicitly constructed, fixed two-node mirror Gaussian-ANM path, not uniformly over nonlinear ANMs.
accepted_scope_caveats.bound_claim: A pointwise local order-testing characterization conditional on the stated mirror-path regularity assumptions.

accepted_scope_caveats.label: Open-ended Question 1 / ass:jacobian-estimability
accepted_scope_caveats.caveat: The feasible-score question is conditional on existence of cross-fitted diagonal-Jacobian estimators satisfying a repaired, explicit nuisance condition; this is not derived from the ANM path.
accepted_scope_caveats.bound_claim: Conditional characterization of exponent attainment for the specified score-contrast template, after its variance-bias and admissible-perturbation requirements are stated.

strengthening_paths.suggestion: Repair Proposition 2 by either requiring r_n=o(C_m(delta_n)) or imposing a stated orthogonal/debiased variance construction that removes the linear covariance error, then prove the exact residual bound.

Make the rare-event lower construction admissible: state M_n q_n^(1/4)=O(r_n), impose the necessary relation between -log r_n and n C_star(delta_n), and limit the converse to sequences for which those constraints can coexist.

Attach the L4 impossibility to a named published SCORE workflow with its exact nuisance premise, or recast it as an internal limitation of the new template rather than a literature-facing negative result.
Extend the mirror calculation to a normalized nondegenerate tangent class with uniform C_m-to-information constants, or prove why C_m alone cannot support such a uniform frontier.

recommended_next_step: Revise Proposition 2’s nuisance-rate and rare-event admissibility conditions, and either anchor its negative claim to a concrete published SCORE workflow or demote it to an internal template limitation; the repaired mirror likelihood kernel remains field-tier.
